LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER DARREN BRANNAN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS - TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 504 BEFORE JUDGE BUSCH ON MARCH 7, 2014 AT 1:30 PM. THE COURT WILL NOT PROVIDE A COURT REPORTER FOR THIS HEARING. IF ANY PARTY WISHES A REPORTER, THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER AND AGREE ON A SINGLE REPORTER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-07-2014 01:30 PM FOR DEPT 504

LAW AND MOTION 504, PETITIONER DARREN BRANNAN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1094.5. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. BASED ON THE COURT'S INDEPENDENT REVIEW OF THE RECORD, PETITIONER FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ALJ'S DECISION WAS CONTRARY TO THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. THE REPORTS OF DR. BAUMBACHER AND DR. MORLEY SUGGEST THAT PETITIONER DID NOT HAVE A PSYCHOLOGICAL DISORDER ARISING FROM PERFORMANCE OF HIS DUTIES THAT INCAPACITATED HIM AND PREVENTED THE PERFORMANCE OF HIS DUTIES AT THE TIME OF HIS RETIREMENT. NOTHING IN DR. PERL'S REPORT CONVINCES THE COURT TO THE CONTRARY. MOREOVER, THE EVIDENCE SUGGESTS A LIKELIHOOD THAT TREATMENT, WHICH PETITIONER HAS NOT CHOSEN TO PURSUE, WOULD AMELIORATE HIS CONDITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Darren Brannan'S Motion For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER DARREN BRANNAN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1094.5

Appearances: Nancy J. Tavernit of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3961) for respondent and opposing party; Christopher Dehner of Jones Clifford (415.431.5310) for petitioner and moving party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Based on the Court's independent review of the record, Petitioner fails to demonstrate that the ALJ's decision was contrary to the weight of the evidence. The reports of Dr. Baumbacher and Dr. Morley suggest that petitioner did not have a psychological disorder arising from performance of his duties that incapacitated him and prevented the performance of his duties at the time of his retirement. Nothing in Dr. Perl's report convinces the Court to the contrary. Moreover, the evidence suggests a likelihood that treatment, which Petitioner has not chosen to pursue, would ameliorate his condition. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
